commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh That this shall be no grief unto you, nor offence of heart unto my lord, either that you have shed blood without cause, or that my lord has avenged himself: but when the LORD shall have dealt well with my lord, then remember your handmaid. Hebrew, "scandal "or sin, for David might defend himself, but ought not to attack or take revenge, like a king. (Grotius) Innocent. Many of Nabal's family were such, and even his fault did not deserve death. Hebrew, "shed blood without cause. "(Calmet) Handmaid, who has suggested this good advice. (Menochius) David was so much pleased with her prudence and beauty, that he afterwards married her.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
